briefing.headline: Rig up a Medium Diving Crankbait — steady pre-spawn approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Medium Diving Crankbait is a strong choice for pre-spawn with clear water and stable conditions.

Wheeler: The DT-6 is the most universal crankbait; it covers the 3-6 foot sweet spot and works from mid-40s water temps through pre-spawn, post-spawn, summer, and fall on rivers, highland and lowland impoundments, and smallmouth lakes.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: The DT-6 is the most universal crankbait; it covers the 3-6 foot sweet spot and works from mid-40s water temps through pre-spawn, post-spawn, summer, and fall on rivers, highland and lowland
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, rock, bluff. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: KVD: Gravel Dog for the 6-12ft mid-range zone in cold water. Round body, super wide wobble, loud rattle. Eats up the bottom — perfect for chunk rock banks, bluffs, riprap around bridges. One of the mo
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, rock, bluff. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: At Santee Cooper, pre-spawn keys were 10 ft and shallower, hard places and rock (hard bottom, rock, gravel), plus brush and stumps. Better if you had both rock and wood but didn't need both

KVD: In cold water, target transition areas where bass will stage before moving to spawning bays. Channel swing ends, flat edges, steeper banks transitioning to flatter areas, where different rock types come together. Chunk rock banks, bluffs, and...

Palaniuk: 'I call it floating a crankbait -- pick a crankbait that dives deeper than the depth you're fishing, reel it so it crawls up and over rocks, then back down the other side. It looks like a crawfish slowly scooting.'
Robertson: In Florida grass (pencil reeds, flat reeds, Kissimmee grass, hyacinth), drop the trolling motor and cover water with swimbaits rather than sitting on one spot.

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable pre-spawn conditions mean fish are staging predictably at 5-12ft on points, channel swings, and riprap banks. They're feeding aggressively as water warms. Work the transition zone between deep winter haunts and shallow spawning areas.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend
